checklist: Optimize space parameters to O(min(M, N))

states.question: What is the space complexity of standard bottom-up tabulated Edit Distance?
states.explanation: Standard bottom-up tabulation maps calculations to a grid table of size (M+1) x (N+1), yielding O(M * N) space complexity.

states.analogy_description: Fill a spreadsheet where row represents chars of string 1, and column represents string 2. Cell (i, j) accumulates the minimum edit cost calculated from top, left, and diagonal cells.

whiteboard_data.title: Tabulation Cell Update
whiteboard_data.description: Calculating cell value from surrounding grid cells.

states.exercise_description: Write a bottom-up tabulated edit distance function `edit_dist_tab(s1, s2)` that fills a grid table and returns the minimum operations.
Write a space-optimized edit distance function `edit_dist_opt(s1, s2)` that only keeps the current and previous rows in memory, reducing space to O(N).

states.prompt: Explain Edit Distance II in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.front: How do you trace back the alignment path?
flashcards.back: By starting at cell (M, N) and moving to the neighboring cell that yielded its minimum value.

flashcards.front: What does a diagonal movement without cost increment represent?
